engines_involved.role: conditional — only fires when user_id is present in event payload
unconditional — fires for every anomaly regardless of user_id presence

registration.legacy_event_type: NOT REGISTERED — only THREAT_DETECTED, FINDING_CREATED, CVE_DISCOVERED are in legacy bus type_map

current_state.summary: Two-engine fan-out with conditional gate on user_id. InsiderThreatEngine.create_alert() is guarded: only called when user_id is present in the event payload. AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() fires unconditionally. Alert severity is hardcoded 'high' for all anomaly-sourced alerts. The insider threat alert indicator is hardcoded as the string 'anomaly_detected'.
current_state.hardcoded_severity: AlertTriageEngine always receives severity='high' regardless of the anomaly's actual severity from the source engine.
current_state.legacy_bus_gap: ANOMALY_DETECTED is NOT wired to the legacy EventBus — only TrustGraph bus registration is present. Engines that use the legacy bus to emit this event type will not trigger the subscriber.

what_works: InsiderThreatEngine.create_alert() correctly receives user_id, source, entity_type as structured evidence.
AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() fires for all anomalies including non-user anomalies (network, system).
user_id gate correctly skips InsiderThreatEngine when the anomaly is not user-attributed.
_safe_call isolation: failure of InsiderThreatEngine does not prevent AlertTriageEngine from running.
Both source and entity_type are passed through to InsiderThreatEngine evidence dict.
what_doesnt: ANOMALY_DETECTED is missing from the legacy EventBus registration (type_map in register_cross_category_subscribers only maps THREAT_DETECTED, FINDING_CREATED, CVE_DISCOVERED). Any engine emitting via legacy bus will not trigger this subscriber.
Anomaly severity from the source engine is ignored — AlertTriageEngine always receives 'high'. A low-confidence anomaly and a certain critical anomaly are indistinguishable at the alert layer.
InsiderThreatEngine indicator is hardcoded 'anomaly_detected' — no mapping from entity_type (e.g. 'impossible_travel', 'data_exfiltration') to a specific indicator type.
No THREAT_DETECTED event is emitted after the insider threat alert is created, so the THREAT_DETECTED fan-out (incident creation, risk entry) does not cascade.
No deduplication — repeated anomaly events for the same user/entity generate multiple insider threat alerts.
entity_type from non-user anomalies (e.g. 'network_spike') has no dedicated downstream handler — it only ends up in alert raw_alert_json.
No test in the Beast Mode suite covers this subscriber.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given ANOMALY_DETECTED with user_id present, both InsiderThreatEngine.create_alert() and AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() are called with the correct org_id.
AC2: Given ANOMALY_DETECTED without user_id, only AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() is called; InsiderThreatEngine must NOT be called.
AC3: Anomaly severity from the event payload must be forwarded to AlertTriageEngine (not hardcoded 'high'); unknown severity values must fall back to 'medium'.
AC4: ANOMALY_DETECTED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map in register_cross_category_subscribers() so legacy-bus emitters are covered.
AC5: entity_type must map to a specific InsiderThreatEngine indicator string (e.g. 'impossible_travel' → indicator='impossible_travel') rather than always using 'anomaly_detected'.
AC6: For anomalies with severity='critical', a THREAT_DETECTED event must be emitted after the alert is created so the incident/risk chain fires.
AC7: A pytest test covers the user_id-present path, the no-user_id path, and severity passthrough.
AC8: Failure of InsiderThreatEngine must not prevent AlertTriageEngine from being called.
